title: Fix cast_assoc mass assignment on nested child
prompt:
The following changeset uses `cast_assoc/3` on a nested `memberships` association, but the child `Membership` changeset itself allows `:role` to be set — so attackers can still become admins by sending nested params:

```elixir
def changeset(user, attrs) do
  user
  |> cast(attrs, [:email, :name])
  |> cast_assoc(:memberships, with: &Membership.changeset/2)
end

# in Membership schema:
def changeset(membership, attrs) do
  membership
  |> cast(attrs, [:team_id, :role])
end
```

Rewrite both schemas so `Membership.changeset/2` does NOT accept `:role` from user input. Create a separate `Membership.role_changeset/2` for admin-only role changes. The parent `User.changeset/2` must pass `with: &Membership.profile_changeset/2` (or equivalent safe changeset). Write the fix to `lib/my_app/accounts/membership.ex`.
tier_rationale: Medium — requires recognizing that cast_assoc doesn't re-protect child-level fields.
